answer: Tumor epithelium with a brisk peritumoral lymphocytic infiltrate, desmoplastic stroma, scattered necrotic debris and extracellular mucin, with residual normal mucosa and smooth muscle at the margin.

certification_scope.concept_level_claim: CONCEPT-LEVEL: the concepts the answer invokes are ones this encoder genuinely represents, uses causally, and that are not staining/batch artifacts. This is NOT a per-slide / per-prediction claim — it does NOT verify the concept is present in any specific input tile, and in the default (no live_ctx) mode it CANNOT catch a per-slide hallucination such as 'tumor' asserted about a stroma tile.
certification_scope.slide_level_claim: SLIDE-LEVEL (live source-intervention): necessity was measured by editing THIS slide's real forward pass (intervened_on_input=true) — a per-slide causal read of whether the readout depends on the concept axis.

questions.is the concept real/causal/unconfounded: YES — this is what the concept-level card certifies
questions.does K-Pro's answer for THIS slide use t: not determinable here — no K-Pro internals

assumptions: encoder-faithfulness: a latent do() moves the model's REPRESENTATION, not tissue biology
K-Pro-faithfulness: K-Pro's pathology inference reads from Phikon/H0-family features the way this probe reads them (unverified from here)
caveat: Certifies model-internal causal use of an answer's concept claims in the encoder's representation — NOT biological/clinical validity.

reasoning_trace.observation: fit a linear probe — L2 logistic regression on the standardized 1024-d frozen CLS — for TUM(+) vs NORM(−), n=600(+)/600(−) tiles, 720 fit / 480 held-out. held-out AUROC=1.000. nuisance intensity screen |r|=0.520 -> PASS
reasoning_trace.interpretation: the concept axis is the probe's unit decision direction in standardized CLS space; the pool separates the concept and, where intensity was suspected, the signal survives a controlled intensity-matched re-test

reasoning_trace.observation: for 3 depths, hooked encoder.layer[7, 15, 23] and projected the diff-of-means concept axis OUT of the CLS token in the residual stream, then let the forward pass recompute to the readout. margin-drop vs 12 matched-random unit directions ablated identically, measured on 10 readout-positive tiles of this slide: mid_early:+0.55(z+23) -> mid:+3.56(z+135) -> readout:+7.78(z+103) (null ~0). necessity score=0.458 = fraction of readout-necessity already irreversible before the readout.
reasoning_trace.interpretation: the model's decision on THIS tile causally depends on the concept from mid-network on; early-layer ablation is recomputed downstream (redundancy/Hydra). intervened_on_input=true — a per-slide causal read

reasoning_trace.observation: added the concept direction to held-out NORM CLS vectors, α=17.77 (one inter-class 'class-width' along the axis): 1.00 crossed the probe boundary to TUM, vs 0.00±0.00 for 100 matched-random unit directions injected at the same α. score=0.510.
reasoning_trace.interpretation: a concept-specific steering axis, de-circularized by the matched-random null (random directions injected at the same α do not flip) — but on its own still near-circular (inject the class-mean-diff axis, score a probe built on it), so it can NOT ground a GROUNDED verdict alone: a genuine necessity (live or non-readout) plus specificity must carry it

reasoning_trace.observation: fit a SEPARATE linear probe for the distractor pair STR vs MUS, expressed its axis in the concept probe's space, and projected THAT out. cos(concept axis, distractor axis)=0.064 (≈orthogonal); concept probe accuracy after the distractor ablation 1.000 (base 1.000) -> target intact. score=0.800.
reasoning_trace.interpretation: the effect is targeted to the concept axis, not general damage — ablating a near-orthogonal distractor leaves the concept probe intact

reasoning_trace.observation: UNCHECKED — single-source data (no site/scanner variation to fit a site probe against). With >=2 sites: fit a linear scanner/site probe and flag if cos(concept axis, site axis) > 0.30.
